Definition: The human-readable name of the originating ontology or database.
The preferred human-readable display name of the concept in English.
Compact URI formatted strictly as PREFIX:ID. Prefix must exist in source_registry.csv.
The logically constructed Fully Specified Name (FSN). Mirrors Primary_Label if none exists.

Permissible_Values: skos:Concept | owl:Class (Primitive) | owl:Class (Fully Defined) | owl:Property | skos:Collection
Definition: The W3C Representational Semantics defining the structural role of the node.
A breadcrumb trail mapping the concept's lineage separated by ' > '.
Alternative labels or abbreviations separated by a pipe (' | ').
A textual definition scope note or exact survey question text.
Examples: A demographic group representing adherents to the Catholic Church.

Definition: Local Concept IDs of immediate parents separated by a pipe (' | ').
The raw local alphanumeric identifier assigned by the source system.
The globally unique resolvable Uniform Resource Identifier. Used as primary key for deduplication.
Boolean flag indicating if the source API contains non-English designations.
The current lifecycle state of the concept within the source system.
Pre-existing mapping links to other ontologies (e.g., skos:exactMatch, owl:sameAs, or text-based mapping notes). Separated by a pipe (' | ').
Local system timestamp when the Python ingestion script generated the row (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
The semantic classification within the domain of religion.
The data lifecycle state of the concept.
